auto_merge.commit_title: Minor refactor of Builtin type flag attributes (#7624)

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

comment_data.body:
Hmm, the comments seem mostly redundant. And the sequence comment risks getting forgotten on future updates. I'd remove them all.
```suggestion
    # Combined type group flags, based on common needs in the code base.
    is_bytes_or_str = False
    is_builtin_sequence = False
    is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray = False
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -295,6 +295,13 @@ class PyrexType(BaseType):
     is_pybytearray_type = False
     is_pymemoryview_type = False
 
+    # is_bytes_or_str is True if bytes or str
+    is_bytes_or_str = False
+    # is_builting_sequence is True if bytes or str or bytearray or memoryview or tuple or list
+    is_builtin_sequence = False
+    # is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray is True if bytes or str or bytearray
+    is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray = False

comment_data.body:
If I'm not mistaken, this is only used once, in a case where `is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray` might actually be more appropriate. It can probably be removed, but we'd then want to add a test for the bytearray case in that corner.
https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/a94adc71cf1aa7d77aeae3f021daacfb56a1c804/Cython/Compiler/ExprNodes.py#L191-L194
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -295,6 +295,13 @@ class PyrexType(BaseType):
     is_pybytearray_type = False
     is_pymemoryview_type = False
 
+    # is_bytes_or_str is True if bytes or str
+    is_bytes_or_str = False

comment_data.body:
To prevent typos, I suggest adding a test that
- checks that all attributes named here exist and default to False on the base type
- validates that the expected types are in the groups (e.g. by reversing the mapping)

That would give us the safety that a test needs to be changed explicitly if we make changes to the groups.
After some investigation, I decided to simply remove `.is_bytes_or_str` and keep the logic of the code as is.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -295,6 +295,13 @@ class PyrexType(BaseType):
     is_pybytearray_type = False
     is_pymemoryview_type = False
 
+    # is_bytes_or_str is True if bytes or str
+    is_bytes_or_str = False

@@ -16,6 +18,41 @@ def assert_widest(type1, type2, widest):
         assert_widest(PT.c_int_type, cenum, PT.c_int_type)
 
 
+class TestBuiltinTypes(TimedTest):
+
+    BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING = {
+        'is_pyint_type': ['int'],
+        'is_pyfloat_type': ['float'],
+        'is_pybool_type': ['bool'],
+        'is_pycomplex_type': ['complex'],
+        'is_pylist_type': ['list'],
+        'is_pydict_type': ['dict'],
+        'is_pyset_type': ['set'],
+        'is_pytuple_type': ['tuple'],
+        'is_pyfrozenset_type': ['frozenset'],
+        'is_pybytes_type': ['bytes'],
+        'is_pystr_type': ['str'],
+        'is_pybytearray_type': ['bytearray'],
+        'is_pymemoryview_type': ['memoryview'],
+        'is_builtin_sequence': ['list', 'tuple', 'bytes', 'str', 'bytearray'],
+        'is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray': ['bytes', 'str', 'bytearray'],
+    }
+
+    def test_set_builtin_type_flags(self):
+        builtin_flags = set(chain.from_iterable(PT.BuiltinObjectType._builtin_type_flag_mapping.values()))
+        builtin_types = set(chain.from_iterable(self.BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING.values()))
+
+        self.assertSetEqual(builtin_flags, set(self.BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING))
+        self.assertSetEqual(set(PT.BuiltinObjectType._builtin_type_flag_mapping), builtin_types)
+
+        for attr in builtin_flags:
+            self.assertFalse(getattr(PT.PyrexType, attr))

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
                self.assertIs(
                    getattr(PT.BuiltinObjectType(type_name, f'c_{type_name}'), attr),
                    True,
                    f"{attr} should be set for {type_name}"
                )
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -16,6 +18,41 @@ def assert_widest(type1, type2, widest):
         assert_widest(PT.c_int_type, cenum, PT.c_int_type)
 
 
+class TestBuiltinTypes(TimedTest):
+
+    BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING = {
+        'is_pyint_type': ['int'],
+        'is_pyfloat_type': ['float'],
+        'is_pybool_type': ['bool'],
+        'is_pycomplex_type': ['complex'],
+        'is_pylist_type': ['list'],
+        'is_pydict_type': ['dict'],
+        'is_pyset_type': ['set'],
+        'is_pytuple_type': ['tuple'],
+        'is_pyfrozenset_type': ['frozenset'],
+        'is_pybytes_type': ['bytes'],
+        'is_pystr_type': ['str'],
+        'is_pybytearray_type': ['bytearray'],
+        'is_pymemoryview_type': ['memoryview'],
+        'is_builtin_sequence': ['list', 'tuple', 'bytes', 'str', 'bytearray'],
+        'is_bytes_or_str_or_bytearray': ['bytes', 'str', 'bytearray'],
+    }
+
+    def test_set_builtin_type_flags(self):
+        builtin_flags = set(chain.from_iterable(PT.BuiltinObjectType._builtin_type_flag_mapping.values()))
+        builtin_types = set(chain.from_iterable(self.BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING.values()))
+
+        self.assertSetEqual(builtin_flags, set(self.BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING))
+        self.assertSetEqual(set(PT.BuiltinObjectType._builtin_type_flag_mapping), builtin_types)
+
+        for attr in builtin_flags:
+            self.assertFalse(getattr(PT.PyrexType, attr))
+            for type_name in self.BUILTIN_FLAGS_MAPPING[attr]:
+                self.assertTrue(
+                    getattr(PT.BuiltinObjectType(type_name, f'c_{type_name}'), attr),
+                    f"{attr} should be set for {type_name}"
+                )
